question_text: Which statement best describes the focus of U11 - Patient/Resident Abuse I: Recognition and Prevention?
answer_a: Recognize possible abuse and neglect signs while preventing harm through resident rights and respectful boundaries
answer_b: Conduct private investigations to decide whether abuse legally occurred
answer_c: Complete hands-on clinical validation for abuse reporting
answer_d: Release a certificate before approval metadata and evidence gates are verified
rationale: U11 is mapped to Module 17 and focuses on terminology recognition prevention rights boundaries and CNA scope rather than investigation or certificate release.

question_text: A resident flinches when a caregiver enters and quietly says Please do not leave me with that person while you notice a new upper-arm bruise. What is the best immediate CNA action?
answer_a: Wait until the end of the shift to see if the resident gives more details
answer_b: Keep the resident safe as directed and promptly notify the licensed nurse or supervisor with objective facts
answer_c: Confront the caregiver in front of the resident and demand an explanation
answer_d: Ask several leading questions until the resident names who caused the bruise
rationale: The CNA should support immediate safety and promptly report objective observations and resident statements without confronting or interrogating.

question_text: Which action stays within CNA scope when possible abuse or neglect is recognized?
answer_a: Decide which staff member is guilty before reporting
answer_b: Ask leading questions to build a case
answer_c: Report the resident's exact words and observed facts without accusation
answer_d: Post a warning about the suspected person in a staff chat
rationale: U11 emphasizes recognition and prevention; CNAs report facts through required channels and do not investigate accuse or disclose confidential concerns inappropriately.

question_text: Which note is the most objective for the Resident K scenario?
answer_a: Resident was dramatic and made trouble during care.
answer_b: Caregiver probably hurt Resident K because the bruise looked suspicious.
answer_c: Resident is lying because details were unclear.
answer_d: Resident stated Please do not leave me with that person; flinched when caregiver passed doorway; new purple bruise observed on upper arm; nurse notified.
rationale: Objective documentation includes the resident statement observable behavior body location and notification without blame diagnosis or unsupported conclusions.

question_text: What does involuntary seclusion mean in the context of resident abuse prevention?
answer_a: Offering a resident quiet time when the resident requests privacy
answer_b: Separating or confining a resident against the resident's will or care-plan direction for convenience or control
answer_c: Closing a door during personal care to protect dignity
answer_d: Helping a resident attend a scheduled activity with consent
rationale: Involuntary seclusion is a rights violation involving unwanted separation or confinement rather than appropriate privacy or consent-based care.

question_text: A resident says their wallet is missing after a staff member repeatedly asked for gift cards and personal passwords. Which concern should the CNA recognize and report?
answer_a: Normal social conversation between staff and resident
answer_b: A clinical symptom that the CNA should diagnose independently
answer_d: Proof that the resident cannot manage any personal property
rationale: Missing belongings combined with pressure for money gifts passwords or access is a possible financial abuse pattern requiring objective reporting.

question_text: What Moodle evidence should be retained for U11 completion?
answer_a: Only a learner's verbal promise that they read the material
answer_b: Copied government forms and signer binders uploaded into the course
answer_c: Clinical or simulation attendance hours for abuse recognition
answer_d: Overview and Book completion, scenario result, reflection/attestation submission, quiz score, and unit completion status
rationale: U11 is online CE only and should retain Moodle activity evidence while excluding copied forms signer binders clinical hours and simulation hours.

question_text: During care a coworker mocks a resident and says call light help will be delayed if the resident keeps complaining. What is the best CNA response?
answer_a: Laugh along so the coworker does not become angry
answer_b: Ignore the comment because no physical injury occurred
answer_c: Protect the resident's dignity and report the verbal threat or pattern through the required chain
answer_d: Tell the resident to stop complaining so staff will be nicer
rationale: Verbal threats humiliation and retaliation risk violate resident dignity; prevention includes speaking up through proper channels and maintaining respectful care.

question_text: Which statement best separates recognition from investigation?
answer_a: Recognition means proving what happened before notifying anyone
answer_b: Investigation is a CNA task whenever the resident trusts the CNA
answer_c: Recognition allows the CNA to promise secrecy until all details are known
answer_d: Recognition means noticing and reporting objective concerns; investigation determines cause responsibility and formal findings
rationale: The CNA's role is to recognize report and document facts while authorized personnel handle investigation findings and formal determinations.

question_text: Which compliance statement is safe for this Draft / Pending CDPH Approval online CE unit?
answer_a: Certificate release remains disabled until approval metadata required evidence and course-level gates are verified
answer_b: The unit includes hands-on validation of abuse response skills
answer_c: Real resident examples should be entered for realism
answer_d: Passing U11 alone authorizes immediate certificate issuance
rationale: The unit must avoid approval claims PHI clinical or simulation credit and certificate release until approval and evidence requirements are satisfied.
